name: Veal Stew w/ Oranges, Carrots & Mushrooms
recipeInstructions:
1. Warm 1 tablespoon of the oil in an 8-quart Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms and cook for 7 minutes, or until they are slightly browned. Remove the mushrooms and their liquid to a medium bowl.
2. In a large plastic bag or paper bag, coat the veal with flour in two batches, shaking off excess. Place the veal in a single layer on wax paper.
3. Warm 2 tablespoons of the oil in the same Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the veal in batches to ensure it is not crowded and cook until browned on all sides. Remove to a large plate.
4. Warm the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil; add onions and cook for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally. Using a vegetable peeler, remove about 5 inches of peel from the oranges, then squeeze out ¾ cup of juice. Set aside.
5. Reduce heat and add orange juice and broth, scraping up any brown bits. Stir in the browned veal, juices, orange peel, and garlic. Cover and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 1 hour, or until the veal is tender, stirring occasionally.
6. Stir in mushrooms with their liquid and carrots; cover and simmer for an additional 20 minutes, or until the carrots are tender. Add salt and pepper. Dinner Plan: Remove half of the stew to a microwave-safe container; let it cool, then freeze for later use. Serve the remaining stew. (Approximate microwave times: Defrost for 35 minutes; Reheat for 8 minutes, stirring once or twice.)
